name: recon
oneLiner: Adversarial claim verification against fetched sources. Outputs a cited research brief.
note: Built in one evening under $20 of inference, per the FieldCraft foundry method.

name: lens
oneLiner: Captions an entire photo library, then answers natural-language queries in about two seconds.
note: Shipped the same evening as recon; the pair is the foundry method's proof case.

name: exa-agent
oneLiner: The full Exa search API surface as one token-lean CLI, self-describing offline.
note: Replaces the Exa MCP: 68 commands where the MCP hands an agent a dozen, for a fraction of the context cost.

name: elv
oneLiner: The full ElevenLabs API as a disciplined CLI — speech, cloning, dubbing, agents, 300+ operations.
note: One JSON envelope per call; no server between the agent and the API.

name: delegate
oneLiner: Hands a bounded task to another model family — isolated workspaces, reviewable diffs.
note: safe/work isolation: safe modes run read-only against a copy, work modes edit the real tree. The dispatcher behind most of the other tools here.

name: law
oneLiner: Legal research built for agent workflows: case law and statutes at tool speed, every citation checked against cases that actually exist.
note: Distributed as the probita package; the command, config dir, and product identity are all `law`. Every field is classified as source fact, deterministic verification, model judgment, unresolved issue, diagnostic metadata, or configuration — no legal conclusion floats free of its basis.

name: Memorum
oneLiner: Memory that survives across sessions.
note: Local-first memory authority (`memoryd`) underneath memorum-harness.

name: Cairn
oneLiner: Persistent, queryable project memory for long-running agent work.
note: Second-generation rebuild of code-briefcase / llm-tldr.

name: AtlasOS
oneLiner: Operating layer for coordinating long-running, multi-agent build work.
note: The coordination layer for long-running multi-agent build work.

name: autonomous-dev-kit
oneLiner: A harness for long-running autonomous AI development: specs, plans, checkpoints, and a deterministic Stop hook that enforces completion before exit.
note: Combines pieces of continuous-claude and the ralph claude-code plugin with original hook and skill work.

name: dev-pipeline-workflows
oneLiner: Claude Code Workflow scripts that turn a feature request into spec review, plan review, parallel worktree implementation, multi-lens code review, and browser QA.
note: Read-only fan-outs run autonomously; steps that edit files in parallel are isolated in separate worktrees. Has a private cross-vendor overlay (dev-pipeline-private).

name: Specgate
oneLiner: A Rust CLI that enforces module boundaries and dependency rules in TypeScript/JavaScript repos, with deterministic CI output.
note: Keeps agents from quietly eroding architecture: tracks policy changes so rules can't be silently weakened, supports incremental rollout via baselines.

name: Coalition Atlas
oneLiner: Private, local-only operating layer for browsing, filtering, and tagging state-policy campaign actors.
note: Runtime artifact is a single index.html — no backend, no auth, no network calls.

name: devslave
oneLiner: The first autonomous builder. Crude, instructive.
note: First entry in the FieldCraft arc: devslave → autonomous-loop → delegate → foundry → recon + lens.

name: autonomous-loop
oneLiner: The /goal architecture — Stop-hook-enforced completion.
annotation: Shipped January 2025 — months before any lab shipped a native agent loop.
note: Second entry in the FieldCraft arc: devslave → autonomous-loop → delegate → foundry → recon + lens.

name: foundry
oneLiner: The build method: decorrelated model families implement, review, and fix in waves under a coordinator that re-verifies every claim.
note: Shipped recon and lens in one evening each. This is a method/skill more than a codebase, but it earns a row — it's the FieldCraft Doctrine's central claim.

name: Forge
oneLiner: A local-first, repo-aware overnight software factory — supervisor, storage, context, adapters, evaluator, benchmark, and browser-eval as a single TypeScript slice.
note: Runs its own quality gate: format, lint, typecheck, tests, plus the full Specgate architectural stack.

name: agentlinters
oneLiner: Lint rules for agent-written code.

name: Desloppify Deep
oneLiner: A coordinator plus eight focused cleanup agents — dedup, drifting types, dead code, cycles, weak typing, defensive fallback, legacy paths, bad comments.
note: Splits cleanup into separate passes away from expensive test commands, then runs the real gate once at the end.

name: wright
oneLiner: Builds and maintains the agents.
note: Spec-to-implementation work-queue runner; maintains a durable external queue of unimplemented spec items and decides when an item is closed by running its acceptance test.

name: skill-cli
oneLiner: Cross-harness skill management CLI for a shared skill library consumed by Claude Code, Codex, and any harness that reads it.

name: polyflow
oneLiner: Runs Claude Code Dynamic Workflow scripts unmodified but dispatches each agent() call to a pluggable worker — Codex, a delegate-backed model, or a mock.
note: Working title, per its own README.

name: OpenClaw HUD
oneLiner: Local dashboard for OpenClaw runtime visibility — agents, sessions, chat activity, cron jobs, model usage.

name: OpenClaw Agent Dashboard
oneLiner: A terminal dashboard for monitoring OpenClaw agent sessions in real time.
note: Hearth-inspired.

name: Memorum Harness
oneLiner: An experimental, Memorum-native agent harness prototype where memoryd stays the local-first memory authority.

name: Personal Agent Benchmark
oneLiner: A custom real-world coding and white-collar agent benchmark, with deterministic floors and an optional LLM-judge ceiling.

name: llm-council
oneLiner: Multiple models deliberate hard calls.
note: Standalone adaptive-deliberation product with its own trial and eval harness.

name: volley
oneLiner: Experiments in what a competent multimodal agent can do at roughly 1,500 tokens per second.
note: Thesis: below ~1s agent turns become ambient; below ~30s full trajectories become disposable and can be run best-of-N.

name: devbox
oneLiner: Reproduces the full Claude Code + Codex + cross-harness dev environment on a fresh Mac as a normal single-account install.

name: CourtListener MCP
oneLiner: MCP server for searching and retrieving legal opinions, court cases, judges, and federal regulations via the CourtListener API v4.

name: mcp-server-porkbun
oneLiner: Lets MCP clients manage Porkbun domains, with dry-run confirmation for risky calls and writes gated behind an explicit env flag.

name: mcp-image
oneLiner: A token-efficient MCP server for generating and editing images with Nano Banana 2 and Nano Banana Pro.

name: quiverai-mcp
oneLiner: MCP server and agent skills for generating and vectorizing SVG assets with QuiverAI.

name: x-watch
oneLiner: Agent-friendly local CLI for read-only X/Twitter recent search, JSON-first, no OAuth flow.
note: Local fallback for automations when Composio's X tool search or execution path is degraded.

name: Polywatch
oneLiner: Inspects individual Polymarket events for bot-like or inorganic trading behavior using public gamma/data APIs.

name: musicctl
oneLiner: Agent-first local playlist operations for Spotify and Apple Music — JSON snapshots, reviewable mutation plans, explicit confirm before writes.

name: findphone
oneLiner: Agent-facing phone/contact lookup CLI so Claude and other agents can reach contacts by text or Signal.

name: Local GLM-OCR
oneLiner: Local-only OCR for PDFs and images using Ollama and GLM-OCR, outputting Markdown or JSON.

name: Obscura
oneLiner: Local-only, keyword-based PDF redaction that blacks out sensitive text, strips hidden metadata, and generates a verification report.

name: Airtable Export
oneLiner: Downloads a read-only local archive of records and attachment files from an Airtable base.

name: Timebar
oneLiner: A native macOS menu-bar app for quick visual timezone alignment.
note: Pre-release SwiftUI app with no third-party runtime dependencies.

name: Olive
oneLiner: A desktop pet for macOS that runs a real Codex CLI conversation and can take actions on the machine.
note: Built on Tauri 2 + React; talks to a sidecar codex app-server process. Hobby project, not an OpenAI product.

name: rlcoach
oneLiner: All-local Rocket League replay analysis tool for performance coaching.

name: Sophon
oneLiner: A task, project, and knowledge workspace that blends human workflows with AI assistance — Supabase-backed, voice capture included.

name: Hearth
oneLiner: A 3D living space and chat interface — an isometric room with ambient lighting and a real-time chat connection to OpenClaw.
note: Where Ren lives.

name: marrow
oneLiner: Local-first health intelligence on Oura Ring data — an analyst, not a dashboard, that surfaces trends, correlations, and n-of-1 experiment results with real uncertainty.
note: Every insight carries an epistemic label: measured, inferred, correlated-not-causal, or speculative.

name: The Studio
oneLiner: A single narrated AI front door that turns a non-technical operator's plain-language ideas into polished images, video, and documents, iterating until it's right.

name: Element Defense
oneLiner: Cooperative multiplayer tower defense — choose an element class, place towers, survive 20 waves.

name: Goff Family Dashboard
oneLiner: A family household dashboard.

name: Wisecoco Bar Dashboard
oneLiner: Always-on, glanceable dashboards for an ultrawide bar display under the main monitor — 50 dashboards across 4 aesthetics.

name: The Control Room
oneLiner: This site — writing, projects, library, and the knowledge graph tying them together.
note: Co-authored with the Claude models it describes. The colophon has the full story.

name: FieldCraft
oneLiner: The site that catalogs the Kit and the Doctrine — the tools and methods behind everything else here.
note: The public catalog for the FieldCraft arc: tools, doctrine, and the method behind the foundry.

name: American Economic Statecraft
oneLiner: The doctrine-institute site alongside erickbrimen.com and prosperaglobal.com.

name: PACTact.com
oneLiner: Campaign site for the PACT Act — map-driven, mobile-first, built for legislative outreach.

name: TPRI
oneLiner: Campaign site for Total Political Risk Insurance — data, narrative, and resources for policymakers and coalition partners.

name: Próspera Radar
oneLiner: A polling-first situational-awareness product for Próspera — watch areas, source profiles, X polling, agent sweep, pending-classification queue.

name: prospera-narrative-monitor
oneLiner: Combined runtime for Próspera narrative monitoring across X and news — polling, classification, queued Slack delivery.

name: DOD AZC Page
oneLiner: A campaign page for the Department of Defense Acceleration Zone Compact effort.

name: AZC Tracker
oneLiner: A Next.js tracker site for Acceleration Zone Compact policy progress.

name: Modern Political Compass
oneLiner: A Nuxt app for placing political positions on a modernized compass, backed by Drizzle/SQLite.

name: erickbrimen.com
oneLiner: Principal site for Erick Brimen — entrepreneur-diplomat, founding CEO of Honduras Próspera.

name: AZC Economic Impact Analysis
oneLiner: Build workspace for a standalone multi-method economic impact analysis of the Acceleration Zone Compact.

name: Crime Data Classification Analysis
oneLiner: Research examining systematic patterns in ethnicity classification within public arrest records using computer vision models.
note: IRB and legal review in progress; software built in anticipation of approval.

name: Blueprint for America
oneLiner: A Next.js policy campaign site.

name: ROOT ACCESS
oneLiner: A production pipeline turning GPT-generated story content into reviewable comic pages, art assets, lettering, and exportable books.

name: Teaching Agentic Development
oneLiner: A slide deck walking newcomers through agentic development end to end, built with Slidev.

name: Excellence Experiment
oneLiner: A controlled A/B evaluation framework testing system prompts on Fireworks AI for hallucination and accuracy impact.

name: Radiant
oneLiner: Open-source shaders and visual effects for the web — drop-in canvas animations, zero dependencies, zero build step.

note: Combines dwp-onboarding and dwp-design as one entry.
